ead.archdesc:
======= RMA04202Geoffrey Chester papers14-22-4202Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
December 20172B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:07:24 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Geoffrey Chester papers
Chester, Geoffrey
14-22-4202
3 cubic feet. 3 cubic feet.
1964-2014. {**Comma Separated List Of Material Formats,End With Period**.} Includes correspondence and subject files pertaining to Chester's career at Cornell. Collection material in English 8942320
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
December 2017
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, December 2017
Date modified:
RMC Staff, December 2017
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Chester was professor emeritus of physics and dean emeritus of Cornell's College of Arts and Sciences. He was an authority on liquid helium.
He received a B.S. from Edinburgh University in 1950 and a Ph.D. from King's College of London University in 1953.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes correspondence and subject files pertaining to Chester's career at Cornell.
Cite As:
Geoffrey Chester Papers, #14-22-4202. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Universities and colleges -- Faculty Physicists. Physics -- Study and teaching. Cornell University. Department of Physics Cornell University -- : Faculty.
